docs: https://dev.timelyapp.com/, full OpenAPI 3.1 reference at https://developer.timely.com/
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Ground truth is Timely's own published OpenAPI 3.1 document, served inline on https://developer.timely.com/ (title 'Timely API Docs'), which declares 106 paths across the full product surface (time entries, projects, clients, users, tags, teams, invoicing, forecasting, timesheet proposals/AI, webhooks, third-party app integrations, OAuth internals, account/permission administration). This connector's domain is time-tracking core data (the legacy connector's own scope: users, projects, clients, calendar/time events); every core read resource in that domain is now a stream. legacy's own 'events' path (`/{account_id}/events`, singular) is NOT present anywhere in Timely's current published OpenAPI document at all — the modern, documented equivalent resource is `/{account_id}/hours` (time entries with linked work items). The 'events' stream is kept exactly as legacy defined it (undisturbed parity; still targets `/events`, which by all available evidence remains a live, working alias even though no longer independently documented), and the modern documented `hours` resource is added as an ADDITIONAL new stream rather than replacing it, so no existing behavior changes. Every mutation endpoint in Timely's real, documented API (clients/projects/users/teams/labels/time-entries create+update) wraps its request body in a resource-named single-key JSON envelope (e.g. `POST /clients` body `{"client": {...}}`, `POST /hours` body `{"event": {...}}`) — this repeats identically across every one of the 6+ mutation resource families reviewed here, well past the ≥3-occurrence recurrence threshold. The engine's declarative write dialect (`body_type: json`/`form`/`none`) can only construct a FLAT top-level JSON object from a record's fields (optionally minus `path_fields`); it has no mechanism to nest that object under a single named envelope key. Implementing any of these writes today would require either (a) sending an unwrapped flat body Timely's API would reject outright (a request failure, not silent data corruption, but still non-functional), or (b) inventing Go in a WriteHook purely to wrap a body — forbidden by conventions.md §1 (bodies are declarative-shape territory, not a hook trigger) and not a fix for the underlying missing dialect primitive anyway. This is filed as ENGINE_GAP (ticket below) rather than worked around; `capabilities.write` stays `false` and no `writes.json` ships. Resources genuinely outside this connector's time-tracking-core domain (invoicing, forecasting, AI timesheet-proposal generation, third-party app/workspace integration, webhooks, OAuth/account administration, day-properties/compliance/capacity reporting) are excluded as out_of_scope below at the resource-family level rather than one-line-per-path, since api_surface.json's own endpoints array would otherwise balloon to 100+ near-duplicate exclusion entries for resource families this connector's domain description never claimed.

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped in a single-key JSON envelope ({"client": {...}}); the engine's declarative write dialect (body_type json/form/none) only constructs a flat top-level body and has no nested-envelope primitive. See scope note above. Not implemented via a WriteHook (forbidden: body construction is not a sanctioned Tier-2 trigger).
ENGINE_GAP: same single-key JSON envelope requirement ({"client": {...}}) as client create; see scope note.
ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped in {"project": {...}}; see scope note.
ENGINE_GAP: same single-key JSON envelope requirement ({"project": {...}}) as project create; see scope note.

permanently deletes a project and all its associated time entries; even setting aside the envelope ENGINE_GAP, this is an irreversible admin-level mutation out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector without a dedicated approval workflow
ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped in {"user": {...}}; see scope note. Also invites a real person to the Timely account (side effect beyond ordinary data mutation).
ENGINE_GAP: same single-key JSON envelope requirement ({"user": {...}}) as user create; see scope note.
permanently removes a user from the Timely account; irreversible admin-level mutation out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector
ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped in {"event": {...}}; see scope note.
ENGINE_GAP: same single-key JSON envelope requirement ({"event": {...}}) as time-entry create; see scope note.
permanently deletes a logged time entry, including its billing/invoice linkage; irreversible admin-level mutation out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector
starts a live timer session (a stateful side-effecting action, not a data mutation on a resource this connector reads); out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector
stops a live timer session (a stateful side-effecting action, not a data mutation on a resource this connector reads); out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector
ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped in {"label": {...}}; see scope note.
ENGINE_GAP: same single-key JSON envelope requirement ({"label": {...}}) as label create; see scope note.
permanently deletes a tag/label and its associations across every time entry using it; irreversible admin-level mutation out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector
ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped in {"team": {...}}; see scope note.
ENGINE_GAP: same single-key JSON envelope requirement ({"team": {...}}) as team create; see scope note.
alternate verb for the same team-update operation already excluded above via PUT /teams/{id}
permanently deletes a team and its user/project associations; irreversible admin-level mutation out of scope for a reverse-ETL connector

billing/invoicing is a distinct domain outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope (users/projects/clients/time-entries/tags/teams); no invoicing stream or write was ever modeled by legacy or claimed by this connector's description
budget/capacity forecasting is a distinct planning domain outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope
AI-generated timesheet proposal drafting/submission is a distinct workflow-automation domain outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope
third-party app/workspace integration management (Jira/Trello/etc. connection config) is a distinct admin domain outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope
webhook subscription management is a distinct event-delivery domain outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope; this connector is a pull-based reader, not a webhook consumer
per-day scheduling metadata, capacity, and compliance reporting are distinct planning/reporting domains outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope
the reporting/analytics API is a distinct aggregation domain (server-computed summaries, not raw resource records) outside this connector's time-tracking-core scope

account-administration resources (custom roles, workflow states, granular permission grants, project templates) require account-admin-level access this connector's OAuth scope is not documented to carry, and are outside its time-tracking-core read scope
account discovery/OAuth-flow/session-management endpoints are authentication plumbing, not application data resources; account_id itself is a required connection-spec config value, not a stream
lists webhook EVENT TYPE metadata for subscription configuration, not application data; paired with the out-of-scope webhooks exclusion above

these top-level (non-account-scoped) paths are reserved/placeholder entries in Timely's current OpenAPI document with zero HTTP methods actually defined on them — dead surface, not a real callable endpoint today
